Autonomy and trust: You will benefit from flexible working hours and ownership of your responsibilities, with a clear focus on quality, rigor, and outcomes.
Purpose-driven culture: You will join a team that values responsibility, precision, and long-term impact over bureaucracy.



Your mission

Your mission will be to structure, protect, and enable SATLANTIS’ space programs from a legal and contractual perspective, while supporting information protection and compliance requirements.

By combining solid legal judgment with structured execution, your work will help safeguard projects, partnerships, and long-term strategic assets.

You will:

Draft, review, and manage contracts: You will handle commercial and project-related agreements, ensuring clarity, risk mitigation, and alignment with business and regulatory requirements.
Support information protection activities: You will coordinate contractual clauses, confidentiality obligations, and documentation flows linked to classified and sensitive information.
Contribute to compliance and regulatory processes: You will support export-control, documentation security, and audit-related activities in collaboration with internal teams and external advisors.



What will set you up for success in this role
A strong legal foundation: A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Law, with a focus on commercial or corporate law, provides the basis to operate confidently in complex and regulated environments.
Experience with contracts in structured settings: Around 2-4 years of experience in contracts, legal operations, or in-house legal roles (especially in technology, industrial, aerospace, defence, or regulated sectors) allows you to add value quickly.
Interest in security and compliance: Exposure to classified information, export control, or sensitive data environments is a strong plus, combined with the motivation to keep learning.
Attention to detail and process discipline: Your ability to manage documentation, track versions, ensure consistency, and follow structured workflows protects both legal and strategic interests.
Clear legal and technical writing skills: You are comfortable drafting contracts, clauses, summaries, and records that translate complexity into clear and auditable documentation.
Professional English proficiency: You can confidently read, write, and negotiate contracts and regulatory documentation in an international environment.



Bonus points if you bring experience with export-control clauses, confidentiality and security agreements, IP-related contracts, or ISO-driven documentation practices.



Ready to protect what makes space missions possible?

If you are a strong legal professional who enjoys contracts, responsibility, and structured environments, and want to grow into information protection and compliance within the space sector, this role is for you.

Apply now and join SATLANTIS in protecting the future of space programs.

El Departamento de Portfolio Management es el encargado de la gestión integral de los portfolios en gestión, cualquiera que sea la naturaleza de los mismos (personas físicas, PYMES o grandes corporaciones, así como REOs) y abarcando desde la recuperación de la deuda, mediante estrategias extrajudiciales de resolución (cash y non cash) hasta la venta de los REOs en gestión o adjudicados tras la compra de la cartera.




El equipo de Portfolio Management trabaja en estrecha colaboración con otros departamentos clave de la organización para diseñar y ejecutar estrategias óptimas y efectivas de resolución de los portfolios, ajustadas a las características específicas de cada deudor, tipo de préstamo o REO en gestión, así como en la optimización de los procesos.




Perfil

El puesto de Legal Manager es el encargado de diseñar y ejecutar la estrategia de recuperación judicial a través del impulso del procedimiento de: ejecución hipotecaria, concursos de acreedores y otros procesos de reclamación de cantidad.




Las principales funciones son:

Redacción de escritos procesales.
Seguimiento de procesos concursales.
Presentación y seguimiento de procesos de ejecuciones hipotecarias.
Interacción directa con el cliente (inversor), asesores externos, deudores, Administraciones, procuradores, administradores concursales, etc.
Preparar y llevar a cabo reuniones y presentaciones frecuentes con la dirección ejecutiva y/o cliente para evaluar resultados.
Gestionar y supervisar el trabajo de otros abogados y personal administrativo del equipo.
Reporting directo al inversor.



Perfil (Requisitos)

Al menos dos años de experiencia en la dirección letrada de procedimientos de reclamación de cantidad, hipotecarios y concursos de acreedores.
Licenciatura o Grado en Derecho.
Buenas habilidades de comunicación.
Nivel Usuario de Excel.
Al menos nivel B2 de inglés.

Onyx CenterSource is the leading global provider of B2B payments and business intelligence solutions to the hospitality industry. Description and Requirements

En Endesa tenemos un firme compromiso con la formación y captación del talento. Por eso ofrecemos un Plan de Becas a recién titulados/as universitarios que quieran formar parte de un modelo de aprendizaje colaborativo orientado a adquirir competencias profesionales.

Para esta beca, buscamos personas recién Graduadas en DERECHO (especialización derecho público) o que estén a falta de su TFG, para incorporarse a través de una beca al área de Regulación, Gestión de la Energía y Derecho de la competencia en Asesoría Jurídica de Endesa en Madrid.

¿Qué hace la Unidad?

La beca es para trabajar en el Departamento de Regulación, Gestión de la Energía y Derecho de la Competencia de la Asesoría Jurídica de Endesa S.A. Se busca personal con un perfil en Derecho Público y conocimientos de Derecho Administrativo.

¿En qué colaborarás?

Las actividades del departamento son variadas y requieren conocimientos detallados en materia de Derecho administrativo y Derecho de la Energía.

Incluye entre otras: llevanza de expedientes administrativos ante la CNMC: requerimientos de información, expedientes sancionadores, recursos administrativos, denuncias, etc. Recursos contencioso-administrativos contra actos y disposiciones generales ante la Audiencia Nacional, Tribunal Supremo, etc. Análisis de normas regulatorias en el ámbito del Derecho de la Energía.

Asesoramiento en materia de Derecho de la Competencia, incluidas entre otras actuaciones, revisión de contratos, llevanza de expedientes sancionadores ,recursos, reclamación de daños en vía civil, análisis de novedades y normativa reciente, etc.

Asesoramiento en materia de Derecho portuario y llevanza de expedientes ante diversas autoridades portuarias. Arbitrajes en el ámbito de gas, PPAs, etc.

¿Qué formación y conocimientos van a ser considerados?

Graduado DERECHO
Conocimientos Derecho administrativo
Inglés C1

¿Qué ofrecemos?

Prácticas en ENDESA, Programa SWING:

Ayuda de estudio de 900,00 € brutos mensuales
Horario: Híbrido - Jornada completa
Centro de trabajo en España: C/Ribera del Loira 60, Madrid

Las becas de Endesa tienen una duración de 12 meses. Constan de dos partes, una Formación Práctica en la Empresa y una Formación Académica.




La Formación Práctica en la Empresa tendrá una jornada completa de lunes a viernes (formato híbrido), un periodo de descanso de 22 días laborables al año y una ayuda al estudio de 900€ brutos. De forma paralela, se realiza la Formación Académica: Máster en Gestión Organizacional, Competencias Clave y Analítica de Datos, de la Universidad Camilo José Cela (120 ECTS), en formato online, cuyo objetivo principal es el aprendizaje y la mejora del perfil competencial.

Job Description:

International Markets (IMa) brings together AXA markets in Latin America, Africa, Turkey, Middle East and Southeast Asia with the determined and exciting ambition to create strong sustainable and profitable growth.
Driven by our culture, International Markets strengthens, energizes and develops innovative solutions and capabilities tailored to the diverse needs of our customers, whilst protecting our reputation, our brand and our balance sheet. To support this emerging market-oriented business model, AXA IMa offers a multicultural and diverse environment (languages, countries, nationalities) and multiple international career development opportunities.

PRIMARY MISSION:

The intern will have to take care of the corporate documentation and deal with the Notary and Commercial Registry to make sure all is in good order. The person that joins the department will also have to discuss and review all type of services agreements. We will also count on him/her to take care of the internal administrative work. The intern may also participate in some M&A files depending on the needs.

In AXA Madrid International Hub legal department, the intern will have the opportunity to work in various an interesting legal files, working with experienced lawyers in a truly international environment and gain a proper understanding of how a legal counsel can add value from his/her position in a multinational company. This experience will be extremely positive and enriching for his/her professional career.


ABOUT THE TEAM:

Highly collaborative, friendly and hardworking atmosphere. We like what we do and we work hard.
We need a team player who is eager to learn and is result-oriented and capable of building relationship easily given we have a number of interactions with other teams of AMIH and the countries under scope.


KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES: What is expected of you?


The intern main tasks will include general support to the legal team in their day-to-day, main activities:

Reviewing all types of agreements (e.g., Service Level Agreements, Share Purchase Agreements, Shareholder Agreements)
Preparing and reviewing documentation needed for internal committees (presentations, memos, summaries, etc.)
Drafting the documentation related with corporate law matters (powers of attorney, proxies, minutes, certificates, etc.)
Handle the documentation and day-to-day relation required with notaries, registries and embassies.
Updating the Corporate Information in the Group Database


PROFILE: What you bring to the role?

Academic background: Law degree or a Double Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration and Law. A specialization in Corporate Law is preferable.
Languages: Spanish, English (Proficiency level, C1-C2) and French is an additional asset.
Advanced profiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
Strong communications skills
Abitility to multitask effectively and adapt to fast-paced environments.
Professional, proactive and detail-oriented work style.


Availability: Full-time internship starting April 2026, with a duration of 1 year (6+6 months)

At AXA, we actively promote Diversity and Inclusion by offering equal opportunities. Possession of a disability certificate will be positively valued.

Role Description

To respond to growing interest and demands for litigation funding in Spain, Deminor is looking for a talented individual to support the growth of our business in Spain and to help us with a peak of activity. We are therefore looking for a Legal Intern to join us for few months initially to help identify & assessing litigation funding investments in Iberia and LATAM. This role offers hands-on exposure to litigation finance, complex commercial disputes, and legal risk assessment in an international environment.




The role is based in Madrid.




You will be part of a growing, international and diverse team of highly experienced professionals and be responsible to:

Assist our Senior Legal Counsel with all cases related work including case management and case review;
Carry out legal analysis work such as reading and analyzing draft legal briefs from partnering lawyers, defendants briefs etc.;
Carry out legal research relating to potential cases and support the case management process from beginning to end;
Provide legal support with all kinds of factual and legal matters on pending or new cases such as fact finding, evidence gathering, building relationships with clients and potential clients to respond to their enquiries;
Review funding requests submitted to Deminor, interact with potential clients, their counsels and experts and negotiate funding terms;
Support general marketing initiatives to help increase Deminor’s visibility in our growing markets. This may entail to proactively contact corporations, law firms and institutions (arbitration centres, etc.) in these markets to introduce Deminor.
Perform general legal and administrative tasks as required.

About you:

We think that to be successful in this role you will be able to demonstrate many of the following attributes and skills:

An outstanding academic record with a major in Spanish law
A qualified student / junior lawyer with 6 months minimum experience in a legal environment (ideally a law firm or a large corporation)
Some experience in participating in a Moot court.
Curious, wants to learn, motivated to go the extra mile
A very hands on / operational approach
Excellent organisation skills
Result-driven
Excellent presentation and communication skills
Capacity to work under tight deadlines

Buscamos incorporar un/a Paralegal para dar soporte al equipo legal en el área procesal, participando en la preparación, gestión y seguimiento de procedimientos judiciales. Es una oportunidad ideal para perfiles con base jurídica sólida, ganas de crecer en un entorno de despacho y orientación al detalle.

Responsabilidades

Soporte en la gestión integral de expedientes procesales (principalmente civil y mercantil, entre otras jurisdicciones según necesidades del despacho).
Preparación y revisión de documentación para procedimientos judiciales: escritos, anexos, poderes, certificados, etc.
Control y seguimiento de plazos procesales, señalamientos, notificaciones y requerimientos.
Tramitación y presentación de escritos en plataformas judiciales (LexNET u otras), cuando aplique.
Coordinación con procuradores, juzgados, clientes y otras partes intervinientes.
Gestión documental: archivo físico/digital, indexación y actualización de bases internas.
Apoyo en la elaboración de informes de estado de procedimientos y en la preparación de vistas.
Colaboración con otros equipos del despacho para asegurar consistencia y calidad en la información.

Requisitos

Grado en Derecho finalizado.
Mínimo 1 año de experiencia como paralegal, legal assistant o similar, idealmente en despacho o asesoría jurídica.
Conocimiento o exposición previa al área procesal (prácticas, primer empleo o experiencia en litigation).
Manejo fluido de herramientas ofimáticas (Microsoft Office / Google Workspace) y facilidad para aprender sistemas internos.
Nivel de español nativo o bilingüe.
Valorable nivel de inglés medio/alto para revisión documental o comunicación puntual.

Qué ofrece el despacho
Incorporación a un despacho de referencia con equipos consolidados en procesal.
Entorno de aprendizaje continuo y participación en casos reales desde el inicio.
Plan de desarrollo y crecimiento dentro del área.
Condiciones competitivas acordes a experiencia.

Titulo: Legal Intern at a fast-growing Legaltech Startup
Descripcion_Completa:
To apply, just fill out this form: https://tally.so/r/nPa2L5

Applications submitted through other channels will not be considered.

Hello!

Were Hoply, an innovative startup on a mission to reshape the immigration industry in Spain. Our digital platform helps foreigners obtain residence and work permits, nationality, and family permits simply, professionally, and 100% online.

By combining expert legal knowledge with cutting-edge AI, we aim to transform a traditionally complex process into one thats smooth, affordable, and human-centered.

Now were looking for a Legal Intern who wants to learn fast, understand from the ground how startups work and what it takes to scale from 1 to 100. Youll work directly with our team and lawyers, helping real people build their lives in Spain while learning the full process from qualification to case submission.

If you want to truly shape something from the ground up this is the place. Your work will have a direct impact, your ideas will drive change, and youll be part of building something meaningful from day one. Were looking for someone curious, empathetic, and eager to grow in a fast-paced startup environment.

What Youll Learn & Contribute To

Talking with potential clients who are interested in starting their immigration process with Hoply, qualifying their cases, and helping them understand how we can support them.
Reviewing client documents and ensuring everything is complete and in the right format before lawyers handle the submission.
Answering client questions with empathy and professionalism through our platform and WhatsApp.
Tracking the progress of ongoing immigration cases and making sure clients stay informed.
Learning about Spanish immigration law by studying our internal procedures, joining consultations with lawyers, and researching new regulations.

Your Profile

Studying the last years of Law or recently graduated, ideally with interest in Immigration or Administrative Law. You can sign a "convenio de beca" with your uni.
Fluent in Spanish and English you dont need to be native, but you should communicate clearly and confidently in both.
Organized, proactive, and detail-oriented you take ownership and enjoy figuring things out.
Eager to learn, build, and adapt fast. You love experimenting and arent afraid to try new tools.
Comfortable working in a digital, fast-moving environment and curious about how AI can reshape the legal sector.
Are excited to grow in a startup where your work truly matters.
Bonus: any hands-on experience with immigration cases or working with clients.

What We Offer

A hands-on learning experience in a real startup, from day one.
Mentoring directly from the founder and other team members.
A chance to play, test and build using some of the most exciting tools in tech and AI.
Being part of the Google for Startups Campus in Madrid a hub for ambitious entrepreneurs.
Flexible hours and hybrid (3 days in the office for the first 3 months, 2 days after that).
Paid internship: 30h/week for the first 3 months at 500. If results are positive, extended to 40h/week for the next 3 months at 750. Exceptional performance will open the door to a full-time role with us.

Hoply helps people from all over the world manage immigration processes in Spain with a blend of expert legal review and advanced technology. Were a fast-growing, mission-driven startup, working with top immigration lawyers and passionate about making bureaucracy more human.

If you're looking to do something meaningful, grow at speed, and be part of a company thats still writing its own playbook wed love to meet you.

To apply, just fill out this form: https://tally.so/r/nPa2L5

Applications submitted through other channels will not be considered.

ABOUT US:


As a world leading provider of integrated solutions for the alternative investment industry, Alter Domus (meaning “The Other House” in Latin) is proud to be home to 90% of the top 30 asset managers in the private markets, and more than 6,000 professionals across 24 jurisdictions.


With a deep understanding of what it takes to succeed in alternatives, we believe in being different in what we do, how we work, and most importantly in how we enable and develop our people. Invest yourself in the alternative, and join an organization where you progress on merit, where you can speak openly with whoever you are speaking to, and where you will be supported along whichever path you choose to take.


Find out more about life at Alter Domus at careers.alterdomus.com


For our office in Madrid, we are currently looking for a Corporate Legal Officer. We offer a young, dynamic, and international corporate atmosphere as well as the benefit of customized training adapted to your needs throughout your career.


JOB DESCRIPTION:




Drafting, management and processing of all types of corporate documentation (Minutes of the different types of Administrative and Management Bodies, General Meetings…) of the relevant corporate structures.
Preparation, monitoring, management and processing of the group's power of attorney structure, appointment and revocation of positions as well as various structural changes.
Follow-up, organization and processing of said documentation before the relevant public and private bodies, Commercial Registries, Notaries and other entities involved in each of the operations. Direct dialogue with the entities described. Coordination of the actions, taking into account the needs in each case and offering practical and effective solutions.
All of the foregoing functions, both drafting and interlocution of any kind, may be carried out by the lawyer both in Spanish and in English, indistinctly. The lawyer must therefore have an excellent level of English.
Pro-actively take on additional responsibilities and administrational tasks as required by your managers or clients.


YOUR PROFILE:




You hold a university degree in Law in Spain.
You have 2-3 year of relevant work experience in the fields of Corporate Law.
Advanced level of English (C1 minimum), both spoken and written.
You possess team spirit, the capacity to build strong client relationships, and take initiative to act without waiting for direction when appropriate.
The candidate must be highly organized, rigorous, and systematic person.
With ambition to work and learn. Proactive and flexible.Ability to work under pressure and meet a range of deadlines.


OUR OFFER:




Permanent contract in our Madrid office.
Fixed salary plus variable compensation.
Flexible compensation policy.
Social benefits.
Ongoing personalized training in a young, dynamic, and international business environment.

We are committed to supporting your development, advancing your career, and providing benefits that matter to you.


Our industry-leading Alter Domus Academy offers six learning zones for every stage of your career, with resources tailored to your ambitions and resources from LinkedIn Learning.


Our global benefits also include:

Support for professional accreditations
Flexible arrangements, generous holidays, plus an additional day off for your birthday!
Continuous mentoring along your career progression
Active sports, events and social committees across our offices
24/7 support available from our Employee Assistance Program
The opportunity to invest in our growth and success through our Employee Share Plan
Plus additional local benefits depending on your location


Equity in every sense of the word:

We are in the business of equity, in every sense of the word. For us, this means taking action to ensure every colleague has equal opportunity, valuing every voice and experience across our organisation, maintaining an inclusive culture where you can bring your whole self to work, and making Alter Domus a workplace where everyone feels they belong.


We celebrate our differences, and recognise that our success relies on diverse perspectives and experiences, as we work towards shared goals and a common purpose. Job Description:
Rakuten TV is a leading streaming platform, available in 42 countries of Europe, which offers premium Hollywood and local content across various types of access: Transactional Video-on-demand (ie buy or rent movies), AVOD (advertising-based on-demand content) or FAST (linear free TV channels with ads).It is available across multiple devices, such as CTV, mobile, web, and Telcos set top boxes.

Our Rakuten Mission
Contribute to society by creating value through innovation and entrepreneurship. By providing high-quality services that help our users and partners grow, we aim to advance and enrich society.
We are looking for an experienced and talented Legal & Compliance Junior Counsel to provide legal advice regarding Rakuten TV´s activities to ensure law compliance and to work with other internal business departments in the negotiation and drafting of agreements with third parties. This person will be part of the legal and compliance team and will be based in Barcelona.
Responsibilities
Support and develop a culture of compliance and integrity at Rakuten TV.
Draft and review compliance manuals and policies, aligning them with the requirements of HQ, as well as with those necessary due to the applicable regulations.
Develop and deliver compliance training sessions for employees.
Draft, review and negotiate all types of agreements (content, partnerships, promotional campaigns, general services, etc.).
Provide legal advice to all areas and activities of the company (IP, advertising, data protection, promotions, consumer law, etc.) as needed and requested by the teams.
Coordinate with external consultants and advisors when needed.
Requirements
University Degree in Laws. Specialization in Compliance is a plus.
At least 3 years of experience in a legal and/or compliance position, preferably within an international environment.
In-house experience at an audiovisual/entertainment, tech, or multinational company is a plus.
Excellent drafting, negotiation, and communication skills.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
High energy and creativity.
Experience working internationally, across different time zones.
Languages: Spanish and English (most of the tasks shall be delivered in English and the candidate must be able to negotiate in English).

About Planet
Planet delivers integrated payment, software and technology solutions to global businesses across hospitality, retail, financial services and mobility.
We are seeking a highly motivated individual to join our growing legal team. Planet is a PE-backed, high-growth business which is expanding organically, geographically and by acquisition. This is an excellent opportunity to leverage your commercial expertise on contractual matters across multiple jurisdictions, develop leadership skills, and play a key role in Planet’s rapid growth.
Role Overview
Planet is seeking a highly organised and proactive Paralegal to support its global Legal Team. The successful candidate will report to the Deputy General Counsel and work closely with Planet’s Legal Directors across various jurisdictions. This is a hands‑on, fast‑paced role supporting a wide range of commercial, regulatory, corporate and operational matters.
The role is ideal for a motivated candidate who enjoys variety, wants exposure to an international business, and seeks to build their career within a high-performing in‑house legal function.
What you will do:
1. Contract Management & Support
Assist with the preparation, review and editing of a broad range of commercial agreements, including:
General terms and conditions, NDAs, MSAs/SoWs, supplier agreements, partner/reseller agreements, technology licences, marketing agreements, data processing addenda, etc.
Support Legal Directors in drafting routine contract clauses and preparing first drafts using templates.
Coordinate internal contract approval workflows and ensure proper execution.
Maintain and update the template repository, version control, expiration/renewal tracking and contract metadata.
2. Legal Research & Memoranda
Conduct legal research on commercial, corporate, regulatory and compliance topics across relevant jurisdictions.
Prepare concise research summaries and internal guidance notes.
Monitor new regulatory developments relevant to Planet’s operations (payments, financial services, data protection, commercial law).
3. Litigation & Claims Support
Assist in managing disputes, claims and litigation:
document collation,
escalation with insurers,
liaising with external counsel,
tracking deadlines and procedural steps.
4. Horizon scanning and regulatory change management
Support the implementation of new regulatory requirements, including document updates and coordination with business owners.
Assist with data protection tasks (e.g., DPIAs, data subject request workflows, record keeping).
6. Operational & Administrative Support
Support the Legal Operations Manager in improving processes, workflows and templates.
Assist with departmental reporting (KPIs, dashboards, audit responses).
Manage legal inboxes and triage requests to relevant team members.
Coordinate meetings, document signatures, and maintain organised digital filing systems.

Who you are:
Essential
Bachelor’s degree in Law or Paralegal qualification (or equivalent).
2–4 years’ experience in a law firm or in‑house legal team (multinational experience preferred).
Strong drafting, proofreading and document management skills.
Excellent organisational ability with strong attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work to deadlines.
High level of professionalism, integrity and discretion.
Strong communication skills and ability to work with cross‑functional teams.
Preferred
Experience in payments, financial services, technology or SaaS industries.
Familiarity with regulatory frameworks such as PSD2/PSD3, AML, and GDPR.
Experience with contract lifecycle management tools or legal tech solutions.
Experience supporting stakeholders across multiple jurisdictions.

Personal Characteristics
Proactive, resourceful and able to work independently when required.
Can-do attitude
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, international environment.
Strong team player with a willingness to assist colleagues at all levels.
Curious, eager to learn and committed to continuous improvement.

What Planet Offers
Exposure to complex international legal work across 100+ jurisdictions.
Opportunity to learn from a highly experienced legal team.
Professional development and career growth within a global business. Hybrid and flexible working environment.
Competitive compensation and benefits package.
Why Planet :
Planet is an equal opportunity employer where diversity is valued, and all employment is decided based on qualifications, merit, and business need.
Come and grow your career in the most exciting, fast paced technology market, with a business that delivers feel-good connected commerce. We would love to hear from you – Apply now.
At Planet, we embrace a hybrid work model, with three days a week in the office.

Reasonable accommodations may be made in order to allow for an individual to perform the essential functions of this role successfully.



About Us

Company Background
Planet provides integrated software, payment and technology solutions for its customers in the Hospitality and Retail sectors and worldwide via a network of global Financial Services Partners.
Founded over 35 years ago, we have evolved our services, delivering an innovative digital commerce platform that puts customer experience first.
With headquarters in London and around 3,000 expert employees located across six continents we serve customers in over 120 markets.

Community: creamos un entorno inclusivo y diverso para todas las personas.

About the Role

TF Bank is a fast-growing digital bank and a pan-European card and payment platform, developing simple and flexible payment and financing solutions through a proprietary IT infrastructure with a high degree of automation.

We are seeking a Legal Counsel to join our team in Málaga. In this role, you will manage legal disputes in Italy – covering customer complaints, pre‑litigation matters, litigation, and regulatory disputes – while working closely with internal stakeholders to reduce dispute risk and improve customer outcomes. You will coordinate with external counsels and relevant authorities, provide pragmatic legal advice across the dispute lifecycle, and help ensure the Bank’s dispute handling processes remain fully compliant with Italian and EU regulatory requirements. You will also contribute to strengthening dispute prevention, governance and customer‑centric resolution routines in a high‑velocity digital environment.

Key Responsibilities
Dispute Resolution & Governance

Contribute to the development, implementation and maintenance of the Group’s dispute resolution strategy, including internal policies, procedures and governing documents.

Ensure that dispute resolution frameworks, contractual standards and customer‑facing practices comply with applicable laws, regulatory requirements and internal risk frameworks.

Assess legal, regulatory and financial risks related to disputes, and provide recommendations to management and key stakeholders.

Manage legal disputes, including customer, commercial and regulatory disputes.

Represent the Company in court proceedings, arbitration and alternative dispute resolution processes, or coordinate and instruct external counsel.

Customer Disputes & Early Resolution

Provide legal support in customer dispute matters, including structured dialogue, complaint handling and early‑resolution strategies aimed at improving customer outcomes.

Support and, when appropriate, participate in direct communication with customers and external counterparties in dispute-related matters.

Legal Drafting & Contracting

Draft, review and update governing documents, templates and guidelines related to disputes, contracts and data protection.

Draft, review and negotiate commercial agreements and other legal documentation.

Data Protection & GDPR Compliance

About the role

The Legal Counsel is primarily responsible for leading and managing procurement-related legal matters within the Amcor Flexibles EMEA business group. In this capacity, the role serves as the key legal partner to the procurement function, supporting strategic projects, contract negotiations, regulatory advice and dispute resolution.




In addition, the Legal Counsel provides broader legal support, advice, and services to the business group. The role covers a broad range of legal matters, including the drafting and negotiation of contracts, litigation and claims handling, as well as commercial contracts, intellectual property, employment, regulatory, competition law, compliance, and other dispute-related matters.




As a key adviser to the business, the Legal Counsel helps protect the company’s interests and rights by providing strategic legal guidance and ensuring that business activities comply with applicable laws, regulations, and ethical standards within the relevant regions.




The Legal Counsel works closely with business stakeholders to support operations and manage legal risk, contributing to both day-to-day legal matters and more complex strategic initiatives. The role is expected to operate independently within assigned areas while collaborating with other members of the Legal function.




This position reports to the Business Group General Counsel and works in close coordination with the wider Legal team.




Key Job Accountabilities

Serve as the primary legal partner to the Procurement function across Amcor Flexibles EMEA, with principal responsibility for procurement-related legal matters.
Draft, review, negotiate, and administer complex procurement agreements, including long-term supply agreements, framework agreements, raw material sourcing contracts, logistics arrangements, and strategic supplier partnerships, ensuring an appropriate balance between cost efficiency, risk allocation, and long-term collaboration objectives.
Lead the management of supplier claims and disputes. Develop and execute legal strategies for pre-litigation claims against suppliers, including delivery failures, quality breaches, or cost escalation disputes. Act as the lead internal liaison for external counsel in formal litigation or arbitration proceedings, ensuring effective case management and cost control. Lead complex mediation and settlement negotiations while preserving critical supplier relationships where appropriate.
Advise on regulatory issues such as: regulations relating to the direct raw materials, and indirect services and products, competition law, ethical supply chain issues, sustainability targets with tier 1, 2 and 3 suppliers, greenhouse gas emissions and SBTs within the supply chain, carbon trading and control, as well other green and industry related regulations.
Act as the go-to legal advisor for supply chain and procurement projects, involving footprint or machine capacity assessment, and raw material sourcing.
Analyze recurring dispute patterns and supplier risk exposure, and proactively update contract templates and procurement processes to strengthen risk mitigation and commercial protection.
In addition to procurement responsibilities, provide broader legal support, including commercial contracts, regulatory, compliance, data privacy, and other dispute-related matters.
Draft, review, and negotiate a range of commercial agreements in collaboration with the business, including sales contracts, confidentiality agreements, terms of engagement for advisers and consultants, and selected M&A-related agreements as required.
Provide guidance and support in the creation, implementation, management of compliance initiatives and develop and deliver targeted training.
Maintain effective working relationships across all levels of the organization and coordinate with other Business Group legal counsels to ensure consistency of approach and effective management of legal risk.



Qualifications/Requirements




Formal Qualifications and Experience
You must be a fully qualified lawyer in an EU Member State with a strong knowledge of EU law as it applies to private commercial transactions, contracts, corporate procurement, competition law, and regulatory compliance.
Minimum of 8 years of post-qualification experience in legal practice, specifically focused on commercial and procurement law.
Deep expertise in drafting and negotiating high-value procurement agreements, with a specific focus on risk allocation, service levels, and long-term strategic sourcing.
Proven capability in claim handling and litigation management. You have a track record of resolving supplier disputes through both strategic negotiation and the management of formal legal proceedings/arbitration.
A proven track record that includes rigorous training within a reputable private practice, complemented by in-house experience where you have acted as a direct partner to business stakeholders.
Prior experience within a manufacturing or complex B2B environment is highly advantageous. You should demonstrate an understanding of the specific legal challenges related to global supply chains, raw material sourcing, and industrial services.
Experience operating within an international or multi-jurisdictional framework. You are comfortable navigating cross-border contracts, international trade regulations, and diverse legal systems.
It would be viewed favourably if you had a strong working knowledge of emerging regulations affecting procurement, including ESG compliance (CSDDD), CBAM, EUDR, data privacy, and modern trade sanctions.



Specific Skills and Abilities



Responsive, Proactive and Ownership-Driven: Able to provide timely and efficient legal counsel while proactively anticipating legal issues and business needs. Takes full ownership of assigned matters from initiation through resolution, driving tasks forward independently and ensuring timely delivery.
Commercially Minded: Demonstrates a strong understanding of business operations and is able to align legal advice with strategic business objectives, balancing legal risk with commercial outcomes.
Pragmatic Approach: Capable of identifying practical, proportionate, and cost-effective solutions to legal challenges, applying sound judgment in dynamic and often ambiguous situations.
Accurate and Efficient: Able to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment, maintaining a high level of accuracy and attention to detail while effectively prioritizing and managing competing demands.
Business Partner Mindset: A collaborative team player who acts as a trusted advisor, building strong relationships across functions and levels of the organization to support informed decision-making and drive the company’s success.

Banco Sabadell es el cuarto grupo bancario privado español, integrado por diferentes bancos, marcas, sociedades filiales y participadas que abarcan todos los ámbitos del negocio financiero bajo un denominador común: profesionalidad y calidad. Su compromiso con la transformación digital, la sostenibilidad y la diversidad se refleja en sus políticas de inclusión y en la apuesta por la formación dual como vía de aprendizaje. A través del Programa CITIUS, Banco Sabadell colabora en la preparación del estudiantado universitario para su futuro profesional, mediante prácticas tutorizadas y orientadas al desarrollo de competencias clave.

El Departamento de Asesoría Jurídica de Banco Sabadell apoya al conjunto de áreas del banco en cuestiones legales y de cumplimiento regulatorio. El estudiantado en prácticas aprenderá sobre análisis jurídico aplicado al sector financiero, documentación contractual y procesos regulatorios.

Objetivos Formativos

Las prácticas permiten al estudiantado adquirir una visión práctica del asesoramiento jurídico en el entorno bancario y empresarial, mediante las siguientes experiencias formativas:

Comprensión del entorno financiero y sus marcos legales: Familiarización con el funcionamiento de las áreas de financiación estructurada y banca corporativa, y su marco regulatorio.

Análisis jurídico aplicado: Desarrollo de habilidades de interpretación normativa, identificación de riesgos y elaboración de propuestas jurídicas bajo supervisión experta.

Redacción jurídica profesional: Aprender a elaborar documentos contractuales (cartas, informes, cláusulas, acuerdos) adaptados a operaciones reales, con énfasis en precisión técnica y adecuación formal.

Aprendizaje colaborativo y comunicación efectiva: Interacción formativa con distintas áreas del banco, práctica en la redacción de informes y presentación de propuestas con claridad jurídica y enfoque práctico.

Gestión del canal de consultas internas: Clasificación y análisis de tipologías jurídicas habituales, desarrollando una primera experiencia en resolución de cuestiones legales frecuentes.

Mentorización y Seguimiento

La persona en prácticas contará con un/a tutor/a especializado/a en Derecho Bancario. Se establecerán objetivos de aprendizaje y reuniones semanales de seguimiento con feedback personalizado para evaluar competencias y progreso formativo.

Titulaciones compatibles: Grado en Derecho.

Requisitos académicos: Estudiante de últimos cursos del grado.

Idiomas: Nivel de inglés B2 (recomendado).

Competencias valoradas: Rigurosidad, comunicación escrita, interés por derecho financiero.

Esta oportunidad formativa permite aplicar los conocimientos adquiridos en el grado en un entorno altamente especializado, con supervisión continua y actividades alineadas con los contenidos del programa CITIUS: aplicación del conocimiento, herramientas de gestión, comunicación profesional, análisis normativo y trabajo en equipo. La experiencia combina aprendizaje práctico, mentoría y orientación al desarrollo competencial, preparando al estudiantado para su futura incorporación al mundo profesional desde una base sólida.

We’re the world’s leading sports technology company, at the intersection between sports, media, and betting. More than 1,700 sports federations, media outlets, betting operators, and consumer platforms across 120 countries rely on our know-how and technology to boost their business.


Job Description


Make the team that changes the way the world experiences sport.

The legal team is an internal service provider that supports Sportradar's fast growth by ensuring a solid and secure foundation. It includes several divisions to ensure integrity and trust are met and exceeded, including Corporate & Employment Law, Privacy, Litigation, Content Operations, Compliance & Risk, Commercial Sales Legal and Sports Partnership Legal.

We are looking for a talented Legal Counsel to serve Sportradar’s Compliance & Risk division, with Intellectual Property (IP) Protection experience. The candidate will provide legal support on addressing and resolving IP infringements, case management, dispute resolution as well as substantial jurisdiction analysis (global) and engagement with various external bodies including sports rights holders, law enforcement and regulators.

The Legal Counsel, IP Protection will be working closely with and as part of an experienced and international legal team as well as Sportradar’s energetic Rights & Revenue Protection team. This role, whilst based in Seville, Spain will require relatively frequent travel across various regional hubs for internal and external meetings.

THE CHALLENGE:

Drafting, negotiating, administering and supervising through to resolution, evidence capture, legal letters and dispute resolution for instances of identified IP infringement
Elaborate, research and provide legal opinions, particularly relating to IP law and the regulatory framework related to the same
Providing general legal advice to operational/commercial/engineering teams of Sportradar
Administrative and other project tasks within the areas of enforcement approaches, understanding and deploying detection technologies, tracking regulatory authorities by market and more
Constant coordination with other internal stakeholders/corporate units (commercial, product, content operations, sports rights partnerships etc…) as well as external engagement

YOUR PROFILE:

IP Protection lawyer with 3+ years of relevant corporate experience (preferred) or in a law firm
Demonstrated knowledge of IP law and expertise in copyright law
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English; fluency in Spanish and any other language is beneficial, but not required
Flexible to adapt to changing priorities and able to work to tight deadlines
Ability to identify and adapt to country-specific regulatory frameworks
A strong understanding of the sports and, ideally, the sports betting, ecosystem including the political, legislative and economic landscape.
An understanding in relation to data and audio-visual content and how such content is both commercialised and pirated would be desirable
Comfortable with ambiguity and able to work autonomously using sound judgment to make difficult business decisions when necessary
Strong relationship management and collaboration skills
Positive attitude; diplomacy and approachability

Asesor/a Legal Faro Santander

Country: Spain

Sobre Faro Santander

Faro Santander es un nuevo proyecto cultural impulsado por Fundación Banco Santander, con el objetivo de convertirse en un espacio de referencia donde arte, cultura y tecnología se encuentren en un entorno abierto y accesible a todos los públicos.

Faro Santander se ubica en el Edificio Pereda, antigua sede principal del banco y una de las construcciones más emblemáticas de la ciudad de Santander. Un edificio histórico que renace ahora con la vocación de convertirse en un ambicioso espacio cultural innovador, integrador y conectado con la sociedad y el tejido cultural de Cantabria.

El diseño arquitectónico del centro ha sido confiado al arquitecto inglés David Chipperfield, ganador del premio Pritzker en 2023. El edificio contará con distintos espacios expositivos y de mediación, un auditorio multiusos, tienda, y una cafetería y restaurante ubicados en su terraza, configurando un entorno vivo, ideado para fomentar la exploración libre y la participación del visitante.

La apertura de Faro Santander marcará un nuevo hito para la ciudad, ofreciendo un lugar dinámico, estimulante y divertido, donde todo el mundo es bienvenido.

El impacto que generarás

Reportando a la Dirección de Finanzas, Desarrollo y Servicios Generales de Faro Santander, la Fundación Santander busca un/a asesor/a legal con ubicación en Santander cuyas principales funciones, entre otras, serán las siguientes:

Asesoramiento jurídico y contractual para la la organización y gestión de Faro Santander, redactando y revisando los contratos de servicios, obras y suministros necesarios para el funcionamiento normal del mimo , así como para el desarrollo de las exposiciones, programas y otras actividades de Faro Santander (préstamos, exposición, licencias, publicaciones, patrocinio, etc.)
Asesorar jurídicamente en todos los asuntos en materia de de propiedad intelectual e industrial, incluyendo, entre otros, acuerdos de licencia de uso de marca, marcas registradas, , derechos de autor, publicidad y redes sociales.
Actuar como asesor jurídico para garantizar el cumplimiento de las políticas y procedimientos internos, así como para identificar y mitigar los riesgos jurídicos de la actividad de Faro Santander.
Supervisar la aplicación y el cumplimiento de la legislación vigente en materia de protección de datos y condiciones de trabajo y salud.
Coordinación con los equipos jurídicos y asesores externos de Banco Santander.

Lo que aportarás al equipo

Nuestra gente es nuestra mayor fortaleza. Cada persona contribuye con perspectivas únicas que nos hacen más fuertes como equipo y como organización. Valoramos quiénes son y potenciamos lo que aportan.

Ensure all PRL documentation is properly archived and compliant with local regulations.

Provide support in preparing reports related to health & safety matters.

3 years of experience in an HR administrative or generalist role.

Experience managing HR documentation and employee lifecycle processes.

Experience working with HRIS platforms (HiBob, SuccessFactors or similar).

Previous exposure to PRL administration or coordination with prevention services is highly valued.

General knowledge of Spanish labor legislation.

Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.

Strong Excel and MS Office skills.

Fluent in Spanish and good level of English.

Job Description:

Analyst – Legal Department at Alantra

Job description:

Alantra is seeking a legal analyst to join our high-performing, experienced, and commercially minded legal team. This is a unique opportunity to grow your career within a fast-paced, efficient, and strategically focused legal environment that values proactivity, creativity and execution.

You will be part of a collaborative, agile, and solutions-oriented legal function that supports a broad range of transactions and advisory work across multiple jurisdictions for both our investment banking and asset management division. With a strong international focus, our team works closely with colleagues in various global markets, playing a critical role in cross-border projects and helping to navigate complex, multi-jurisdictional legal landscapes.

Join a globally connected financial services group where legal excellence and business strategy go hand in hand.

Key responsibilities :

We are looking for a proactive professional with a forward-thinking perspective, eager to learn, with ability to work in a fast-paced and international environments, excellent verbal and written communication skills, and rigorous attention to detail and process.

The Legal Department of Alantra is looking for a young professional to joint its team to support and develop its Spanish and international entities in all related corporate matters, with a specific focus in asset management legal matters which may comprise matter such as:

Provide legal support on the structuring, launch, and maintenance of investment funds in multiple jurisdictions.
Drafting, reviewing, and negotiating a broad range of agreements regarding our asset management business: including fund documentation, investment management and advisory agreements, distribution arrangements, and service provider contracts.
Collaborate with compliance, risk, operations, and investment teams to address legal issues across product development, distribution, and portfolio management activities.
Liaise with external counsel and regulators in various jurisdictions as needed.
Preparation of regulatory filings regarding the securities market and asset management with national and international supervisory authorities, including for instance, the incorporation of investment vehicles or asset managers.
Monitor legal and regulatory developments affecting asset management and advise on potential business impact and implementation strategies.
Support the development of templates for legal documents and other materials to enable the legal team to operate as effectively and efficiently as possible.

Participate in digitalization and innovation projects of the Legal Department

Requirements:

Role requirements:

Spanish qualified lawyer with 1-2 years of experience, preferably in the funds/asset management area of a law firm or in the legal department of companies within the sector, such as financial institutions, fund management companies, or similar entities.
Knowledge of financial regulation and equity capital markets will be highly valued.
Excellent written and verbal presentation skills in Spanish and English. Other languages will be highly valued.
Excellent interpersonal communication skills.
Ability to manage multiple priorities, and provide pragmatic, business-oriented legal advice under tight deadlines.
Demonstrated ability partnering with international colleagues in different jurisdictions – Experience collaborating with senior stakeholders.
Commitment, rigorous detail-oriented, attitude and ability to show initiative at work, with the ability to contribute with your own ideas. Team-oriented, flexible, proactive, and solution-driven.

Application Details:

Start date: As soon as possible.

Location: Madrid, Spain.

The role : Junior Legal Counsel (M/F/X)
Your Responsibilities:
Work on a broad range of legal matters in close cooperation with internal teams based in Spain, France, Switzerland and Germany
Review and verify legal documentation provided by customers and subcontractors
Draft and renew commercial agreements, as well as update our internal databases
Support the review of commercial and corporate agreements
Contribute to the drafting, updating and management of internal legal policies
Contribute to legal research on various topics in order to support other departments and monitor legal developments impacting the business
Support the development and monitoring of GDPR compliance
Contribute to the deployment and follow-up of the Group’s CSR action plan
Perform administrative tasks and provide general support to the Legal Department, including assistance in the preparation of presentations and training materials
Translate and harmonize legal documents (agreements, internal legal policies) in several languages

What we are looking for:

A candidate who holds a Master’s degree in Law
A candidate with 1–2 years of professional experience within the Legal Department of a company, ideally in an international environment
Fluency in both English and French is required; Spanish and/or German would be a plus
A proactive, reliable and solution-oriented legal professional
Someone comfortable working autonomously in a dynamic and demanding international environment
A well-organized and detail-oriented profile, with strong analytical and communication skills
A candidate able to interact effectively with external partners




We offer:

A competitive compensation plan (salary, meal vouchers, health insurance, paid sport membership…)

Various missions and projects that will allow you to have a real impact on the company

The ability to work autonomously and to drive new initiatives

A great office in the heart of Madrid

Flexible working conditions (hours, teleworking, ...)

A question? Let's talk about it!

En Zaragoza, buscamos un/a administrativo/a financiero/a para unirse a una destacada empresa del sector metal. Tu labor será fundamental en el día a día, abarcando desde la gestión de llamadas telefónicas y la supervisión del correo electrónico, hasta el manejo detallado de albaranes.

Tendrás la oportunidad de desempeñar diversas funciones administrativas y financieras, contribuyendo activamente al buen funcionamiento de la compañía. Este puesto requiere una persona organizada y proactiva en el manejo de las tareas diarias.

La jornada es completa, de 40 horas semanales, distribuidas de lunes a viernes. El horario será en turno de mañana, cubriendo el periodo entre las 07:30 y las 14:30, siempre respetando los descansos legales establecidos.


Experiencia mínima de 1 año en un puesto de trabajo igual o similar al descrito.
Buscamos a una persona proactiva y polivalente.
Valorable residencia cercana al puesto de trabajo.
GM en finanzas o similares.

About us

DICEUS is a strategic technology partner for custom software development. Since 2011, we have accomplished more than 150 projects and contributed to our client’s growth in banking, fintech, insurance, healthcare, and other niches. We help SMBs and enterprises overcome business challenges with the right technology solutions.

About the role:

We are looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to take full ownership of all legal processes within the company in the fields of IT outsourcing, outstaffing, Dedicated Team, and product development.

Main Responsibilities:

Client Contracts:

Drafting, reviewing, negotiating, and managing contracts (outsourcing, outstaffing, dedicated team, product-related contracts).
Managing the contract negotiation process: internal coordination and external discussions with clients.
Ensuring compliance with international regulations (EU, US), including: GDPR, DORA, CCPA, SOX, HIPAA, etc.
Advising on software licensing models and structuring the most beneficial conditions for the company.
Defending the company’s interests during negotiations with clients and handling objections.

Partner and Vendor Contracts:

Drafting and managing partnership agreements with various types of partners.
Managing contracts with vendors: drafting, negotiation, and compliance monitoring.
Optimizing templates and processes to increase efficiency.

Contracts with Company Personnel:

Drafting and managing B2B contracts with contractors (current and potential).
Providing legal support for relationships with contractors.

Interaction with Finance Function:

Coordinating payment terms and tax clauses (VAT / Permanent Establishment risk in applicable jurisdictions).
Supporting sanctions and export control checks of counterparties.
Determining and agreeing on liability limits.
Participating in matters related to opening and closing legal entities, preparation of statutory documents, and registration support.

Legal Processes:

Developing and updating contract templates.
Managing contract approval workflows across the company.
Organizing contract storage.
Monitoring contract expiration dates.
Handling escalations and dispute resolution for all contract types.
Managing legal proceedings in court or arbitration (e.g., non-payment, claims from counterparties).

Product Legal Support:

Providing legal support for the company’s product portfolio.
Participating in defining legal conditions for new solutions.
Monitoring IT law trends and adapting processes to new regulatory requirements.

Requirements:

4–6 years of legal experience, including:
Proven experience in international IT outsourcing/outstaffing companies,
Experience in product IT companies is highly desirable (product background is a strong advantage).
Excellent knowledge of contract and corporate law, as well as international regulations.
Proven track record in negotiations and defending company interests.
English proficiency at B2 (Upper-Intermediate) or higher, allowing for confident verbal and written negotiations.
Experience with different software licensing models.
Skills in structuring and optimizing legal processes.
Strong communication skills, proactivity, and business-oriented mindset.

Nice-to-Have:

Knowledge of fintech, insurtech, or other regulated industries.
Experience with LegalTech tools implementation.

Why Join Us?

Opportunity to work on cutting-edge insurance technology solutions.
Exposure to leading insurance companies, brokers, and MGAs..
Competitive compensation and performance-based incentives.

If you are passionate about building and delivering world-class insurance technology solutions, we would love to hear from you!

Apply now and be a part of the DICEUS innovation journey!

Se busca a personas recién graduadas en Derecho para un proyecto enfocado en la administración pública del siglo XXI. Esta oportunidad está diseñada para jóvenes profesionales que buscan nuevos desafíos en este ámbito.

Los requisitos incluyen poseer un Grado en Derecho. Se dará preferencia, para dos de las plazas, a aquellos con un Doble Grado en Derecho y Administración y Dirección de Empresas, y para la plaza restante, se valorará el Doble Grado en Derecho y Relaciones Laborales. Es indispensable tener entre 18 y 29 años y estar inscritos en el Sistema Nacional de Garantía Juvenil.

Se ofrece un contrato laboral temporal de un año a jornada completa. El horario será de lunes a viernes, de 08:00 a 15:00 horas. El salario bruto mensual será de 1.929 €, incluyendo la prorrata de las pagas extraordinarias.

¡Hola!

Soy Mariana, recruiter de esta posición. Viajar es una de mis mayores alegrías, y lo que lo hace aún más especial es poder vivir esas experiencias con mi familia y nuestra querida gata Rosita.



Quizás somos la décima oferta que lees hoy, en el sofá con Netflix de fondo, o en pleno proceso de cambios… y todas te parecen iguales.

¡Te seré clara, en Zurich no somos perfectos, pero molamos bastante! Y si quieres saber por qué, sigue leyendo.

Antes de nada, GRACIAS por interesarte en nuestra compañía. Sabemos que la búsqueda de nuevas oportunidades suele ser un reto, pero… ¡no te preocupes que todo llega!

¡Vayamos al grano! Intentaré resumirte en 2 puntos nuestro ADN:

Nos encanta el voluntariado, pero preferimos ser activistas del cambio. ¿Cómo? Poniendo nuestro foco en jóvenes que están en riesgo de exclusión social o luchando contra el cambio climático con acciones como la reforestación de bosques en Madrid y en el Parque de Doñana. En 2024 hicimos más de 20.000 horas de activismo.
En Zurich, lo decimos alto y claro: estamos orgullosos de nuestros colores y creemos que un equipo diverso marca la diferencia. Aquí no hablamos de discapacidad, hablamos de habilidad. Que puedas ser tú mismo/a es nuestra prioridad, sin armarios que se interpongan entre nosotros y sin techos de cristal. El 50% de nuestros puestos de responsabilidad están ocupados por mujeres (incluido el Comité de Dirección).
¿Sabes que producimos nuestros propios podcasts sobre identidad sexual? Estamos construyendo una compañía en la que todas las personas son bienvenidas.



Necesitamos incorporar de manera temporal dentro del Departamento Legal de Deutsche Zurich Pensiones un/a Asistente Legal - Contrato de Prácticas.




Importante:

Esta oferta se enmarca en la modalidad de Contrato Formativo para la Obtención de Práctica Profesional, regulado por el artículo 11 del Estatuto de los Trabajadores.
Para poder inscribirte, debes cumplir los siguientes requisitos:


Haber finalizado estudios universitarios, máster o certificados oficiales en los últimos 3 años (o 5 años en caso de diversidad funcional).


No haber tenido otro contrato formativo en la misma titulación y sector.



Y te preguntarás: Marian ¿cómo será mi día a día con la camiseta azul? Me encanta que me hagas esta pregunta (yo también me la hice en su momento):



Comunicación e interlocución con organismos públicos: Registro Mercantil, Juzgados, Dirección General de Seguros y Fondos de Pensiones
Implementación, gestión, tramitación, control y seguimiento de planes y fondos de pensiones desde una perspectiva legal.
Monitorización y análisis de nueva normativa con impacto directo y/o indirecto en la compañía.
Resolución extrajudicial de reclamaciones y requerimientos.
Asesoramiento jurídico al resto de Departamentos: consultas internas y externas, preparación de documentación legal, etc.
Desarrollo de políticas de cumplimiento normativo.



A estas alturas, te estarás preguntando, ¿Cómo sé si encajo?



No te preocupes, no te voy a pedir que salves el planeta ni que cumplas el 100% de los requisitos.

Graduado/a en Derecho,
Conocimientos en Derecho Societario.
Habilidades comunicativas y de priorización.
Capacidad para desarrollar varias tareas a la vez de forma proactiva y eficaz.
Valorable Máster de Especialización en Derecho Mercantil.



Ahora mismo, o estás con la emoción de cuando un pantalón te entra perfecto o estás pensando: “esto no es para mí” (si no es para ti, no pasa nada, ya nos volveremos a encontrar en el futuro). Puedes hacer de celestina compartiendo mi contacto con quien creas que pueda encajar.




En Zurich somos muy disfrutones, nos gusta vivir la vida. Ya me contarás cómo podemos hacerte feliz, pero a mí me encanta poder conciliar mi trabajo con mi vida personal. Aquí la flexibilidad horaria y la desconexión digital son reales.


#PorSerDeZurich, puedes escoger los beneficios que mejor se adaptan a tus necesidades. Ya sabemos que, para gustos, los colores.


Te ofrecemos un modelo híbrido mega flexible para trabajar cómo y dónde quieras dentro de España. Te ayudamos con un bono de oficina en casa.
Confiamos en la filosofía de aprender haciendo para que puedas moverte a otras áreas a nivel local o internacional gracias a nuestro programa “Talento en Movimiento”. Y para que sigas con tu desarrollo y crecimiento: clases de inglés, formación y programa de mentoring personalizado.
Queremos romperte los esquemas de la retribución flexible. Con nuestra bolsa de beneficios, podrás elegir lo que mejor se amolde a ti y a tus necesidades: seguro médico, guardería, formación, tickets restaurante... ¡y mucho más!
Nos gusta dibujar sonrisas y en Zurich sabemos que es más fácil de lo que parece, por eso somos activistas. ¿Y por qué no dedicar un día entero a la causa que más te motive?
Referral bonus si nos recomiendas algún talento como tú. No buscamos a nadie perfecto, pero si conoces a alguien que mola, compártele mi contacto, me encantará conocerle.
¡Si tienes espíritu deportivo, en Zurich vamos a por el oro! Maratones, carreras, descuentos en gimnasio y mucho más. 3,2,1… ¡calienta que salimos!
¿Eres más de playa o de montaña? Tendrás 25 días de vacaciones, más el día del seguro y el día de tu cumpleaños para que disfrutes de lo que más te gusta. Pero que no te engañen, desconexión de verdad. Porque sí, todos nos merecemos un descansito y disfrutar de la vida, que son dos días.
En Zurich, pensamos en tu futuro y te ofrecemos plan de jubilación y seguro de vida.
¡Buscamos los mejores precios para ti! Tendrás acceso a miles de descuentos en productos aseguradores y bancarios.


Si has llegado hasta aquí, GRACIAS por leerme. ¿Qué te ha parecido? ¿Molamos o no?


Si has sentido mariposas en el estómago y crees que encajamos, manda señales de humo, o el CV, si no te va bien lo del humo.


Y para ir terminando, ¿quieres saber qué opinan otras personas que forman parte de Zurich? Yo te diría que seguimos estando en lo más alto en Top Employers España como carta de presentación, pero te animo a echar un vistazo a nuestro perfil de Glassdoor para que sean ellas quiénes te lo cuenten. Sin trampa ni cartón.


Espero conocerte muy pronto.


Mariana Malnis

Descripción:

¿Qué le pides a un trabajo? ¿En qué valores te fijas para buscarlo?

Si quieres formar parte de una compañía volcada en las personas, tienes inquietud por ayudar a mejorar la calidad de vida de los demás y valoras realizarte en un ambiente laboral excepcional, con excelentes políticas de conciliación, beneficios sociales y oportunidades de crecimiento y estabilidad, has encontrado tu lugar.

El grupo ARAG es una empresa alemana con sede en Dusseldorf, formada por un equipo humano extraordinario de más de 4.000 personas repartidas por 19 países.

Además, es líder del sector de los seguros de Defensa Jurídica y Asistencia en Viaje en España y cuenta con más de 20 millones de clientes en España.

Buscamos para el Dpto. de Administración General a 3 personas resolutivas, comprometidas y responsables, con competencias para trabajar en equipo y con una clara orientación de servicio.


¿Qué hacemos en Administración?

Es el encargado atender las llamadas de nuestros clientes, mediadores y Comerciales sobre asuntos administrativos: emisiones de pólizas, estudios de las pólizas de ARAG Alquiler, bajas o modificaciones, consulta y liquidación de recibos, acceso a la web para los mediadores, consultas sobre las coberturas de las pólizas, recibos, etc.


¿En qué consiste el puesto?



Realizar el alta de pólizas, emisión de anexos, suplementos y cualquier tarea administrativa derivada de la emisión o vencimiento de una póliza.
Recepcionar y atender todas las llamadas telefónicas resolviendo cualquier consulta que puedan tener los asegurados/as o mediadores respecto al producto o póliza.




Requisitos:

Imprescindible:



Experiencia previa en funciones administrativas y atención telefónica.
FP en Administración o similar.

Competencias: Capacidad analítica, orientación al cliente, orientación a resultados, empatía, asertividad, trabajo en equipo, planificación y organización, compromiso con el trabajo y la organización.

Formar parte de una empresa líder en el sector, consolidada y en fase de expansión.

Descripción


¿Te imaginas comenzar tu carrera profesional en el mundo del cumplimiento normativo de la mano de una compañía que apuesta por el desarrollo y la especialización desde el primer día?


En nuestra organización, cada historia comienza con una oportunidad. Como la de Laura, una joven graduada en Derecho que, sin experiencia previa, decidió dar el salto y apostar por un entorno donde su crecimiento no dependiera de los años, sino de su actitud, compromiso y ganas de aprender. Hoy, Laura lidera proyectos de cumplimiento en compañías del sector público y privado, ha participado en auditorías clave y se ha formado en redacción de políticas, automatización de documentación y asesoramiento legal estratégico.


Ahora queremos escribir una nueva historia. La tuya.


Buscamos a una persona con formación universitaria en Derecho, con ganas de aprender y crecer en un entorno profesional dinámico. No requerimos experiencia previa —aunque se valorará—, pero sí buscamos una mentalidad proactiva, capaz de asumir retos, trabajar bajo presión y adaptarse a múltiples proyectos.


¿Qué harás?



Implementarás y gestionarás programas de cumplimiento normativo.
Participarás en auditorías y en el desarrollo de nueva documentación interna.
Asesorarás a clientes en el cumplimiento de normativas clave.
Aprenderás a automatizar procesos y mejorar la eficiencia de los servicios ofrecidos.


¿Qué te ofrecemos?


Contrato indefinido desde el primer día.
Salario anual de 20.000€ + 10 % de Bonus, más beneficios como retribución flexible y formación continua.
Horario flexible y opción de teletrabajo 2 días a la semana.
Formación y acompañamiento real para que puedas adquirir autonomía y afrontar tus propios proyectos a partir de los 6-8 meses.
Clientes de sectores diversos, tanto públicos como privados, que enriquecerán tu experiencia desde distintos ángulos.


¿Estás preparado/a para empezar tu propia historia con nosotros?


Entonces no lo dudes: inscríbete y da el primer paso. Aquí no solo buscamos currículums, buscamos actitud, compromiso y ganas reales de crecer en una profesión con futuro.

Requisitos

¿Qué necesitamos de ti para empezar esta historia con nosotros?


No buscamos al profesional perfecto. Buscamos a alguien con ganas de aprender, crecer y aportar valor desde la autenticidad y el compromiso. Queremos que esta sea tu primera gran oportunidad… o el inicio de un nuevo camino profesional.


Por eso, solo te pedimos tres cosas esenciales:


Que tengas el Grado en Derecho finalizado.

Que vivas en la provincia de Girona (el puesto está en Riudellots de la Selva, pero nos adaptamos siempre que tú puedas hacerlo también).

Que llegues con ganas. La experiencia no es un requisito, pero la actitud sí.


Nos gustará si además...


Conoces temas como Protección de Datos, Comercio Electrónico, Compliance, Igualdad, Prevención de Riesgos Laborales o incluso Prevención de Blanqueo de Capitales. No te preocupes si no los dominas aún: lo importante es que te interesen y tengas curiosidad por aprenderlos.


¿Qué tipo de persona encaja con nuestro equipo?


Buscamos una mentalidad abierta y flexible, capaz de adaptarse a un entorno en evolución. Alguien que sepa organizarse, que busque la calidad en su trabajo, y que entienda que los resultados no llegan solos: hay que construirlos, día a día, con compromiso.


Estas son las competencias que más valoramos:




Adaptabilidad y flexibilidad
Autonomía e iniciativa
Autocontrol emocional
Orientación a resultados
Planificación y organización
Creatividad e innovación
Compromiso y responsabilidad
Comunicación efectiva
Enfoque al cliente, tanto interno como externo

Idiomas y herramientas

Sobre PLD Space:

PLD Space es una compañía internacional de servicios espaciales con ingeniería y fabricación propia. Se dedica al transporte al espacio de satélites y personas, integrando verticalmente ingeniería, ensayos, fabricación y operaciones de sus cohetes reutilizables y sostenibles. Con sede central en Elche (España) y fundada en 2011 por Raúl Torres y Raúl Verdú, PLD Space se ha consolidado como un referente en el sector de lanzadores espaciales a nivel global. Su familia de lanzadores MIURA y su cápsula tripulada LINCE posicionan a la empresa como líder de la soberanía tecnológica europea en el transporte espacial, cubriendo todo el rango de misiones espaciales.

Con un equipo de más de 400 empleados y 220 millones de financiación total, PLD Space cuenta con el mayor banco de pruebas privado de Europa, situado en Teruel (España), y opera en dos bases de lanzamiento: El Arenosillo, en Huelva (España), y el Centro Espacial Guayanés (CSG), en Kourou (Guayana Francesa). En total, dispone de más de 188.000m2 de infraestructuras industriales bajo gestión.

El trabajo en equipo, el coraje, la determinación y la ambición permiten a PLD Space garantizar una capacidad autónoma e independiente de acceso al espacio para satélites basada en la fiabilidad a un precio competitivo a nivel global. La compañía es capaz de ofrecer los mejores servicios de lanzamiento a operadores comerciales de satélites, instituciones, agencias espaciales y entidades gubernamentales nacionales e internacionales.

Santander ( www.santander.com ) is evolving from a global, high-impact brand into a technology-driven organization , and our people are at the heart of this journey. WE VALUE YOUR IMPACT

Estamos buscando a un/a Técnico/A Administrativo enfocado/a en la Gestión de Trámites (contrato temporal) para formar parte de uno de nuestros equipos en Qida.





¿Quiénes somos?

Somos un equipo de más de 200 personas talentosas trabajando por un mismo objetivo. Hemos atendido a más de 19.000 familias en los últimos 7 años, cerrando el 2024 con una facturación bruta de más de 75 millones de euros multiplicando por 2x los resultados de 2023





Qida es una start-up privada y social de alto crecimiento en el sector de la atención domiciliaria, fundada el 2018 con una misión social y doble:

Primero, queremos construir el servicio de atención domiciliaria de más calidad del mundo: experto, humano, centrado en la persona y utilizando las nuevas tecnologías;
Segundo, queremos devolver el estatus social a los cuidadores y cuidadoras.


La visión de Qida es hacer sostenible el sistema sanitario y social a escala global ayudando a las personas en situación de dependencia y/o cronicidad a vivir Más tiempo, en Mejor estado de salud y Más calidad de vida en casa» (lo que nosotros le decimos la triple M»).


Qida está cambiando el mundo, siendo líder absoluto en calidad del sector (NPS de 4.8/5) y en tecnología. Qida aporta un enfoque centrado en la atención personalizada en el domicilio basado en la tecnología, la calidad y el impacto social.


En Qida somos la primera empresa de nuestro sector en España en ser B Corp. Con este reconocimiento se evidencia que nos preocupamos por generar un impacto positivo en el cuidado de las personas mayores, además de que nuestros estándares en términos de desempeño social, transparencia y responsabilidad legal están en su más alto exponente.





Qida elige no escoger entre impacto social y rentabilidad; Qida persigue ambas cosas al mismo nivel. Qida va a cambiar la vida de las personas con calidad, personalización y tecnología, con impacto económico y social.

Con un equipo ubicado en Madrid, Barcelona, A Coruña, Valencia, Sevilla, Palma de Mallorca, Málaga y Las Palmas de Gran Canaria, el Departamento de Dispute Resolution & Insolvency (Legal Procesal, insolvencia y reestructuraciones) de KPMG Abogados ofrece asesoramiento legal integral a clientes nacionales e internacionales, en la solución de toda clase de disputas, escenarios pre-litigiososos y de distress.


En concreto, nuestra amplia experiencia nos permite abarcar desde DRI, entre otras, las siguientes funciones:


Asistencia jurídica completa, agotando todas las instancias del ámbito jurisdiccional que resulten precisas, tanto nacionales como europeas, en defensa de los intereses de nuestros clientes.
Asistencia jurídica completa en procedimientos arbitrales.
Asistencia jurídica en la fase pre-litigiosa.
Asistencia jurídica en toda clase de conflictos en materia contractual, societaria, financiera, competencia, de seguros, responsabilidad de administradores, construcción y urbanismo, patrimonial, y disputas relacionados con contratos de agencia, licencia, concesión, distribución y franquicia, etc
Asesoramiento legal en materia concursal, tanto a acreedores como deudores e inversores, en operaciones de M&A, compraventa de unidades productivas, concursos de acreedores y procesos de reestructuración, interviniendo en la redacción de documentos, escritos procesales y asistencia a vistas.
Asesoramiento a administradores/consejos de administración.
Asesoramiento legal en transacciones, interviniendo en la determinación de la estrategia, mitigación o eliminación de riesgos.
Asesoramiento legal y apoyo al resto de áreas de KPMG Abogados, y de consultoría de KPMG Asesores.

THE DIFFERENCE YOU MAKE

Legal advice to traditional, synthetic securitisations, warehousing and lending services as structurer / placement agent or lender, as appplicable. Drafting and /or reviewing relevant legal documentation including terms sheets, prospectuses, escrituras, final terms /notes Ts&Cs, subscription agreements, legal opinions, auditors' letters, etc.
Assisting with the review and analysis of Volcker requests.
Legal advice to other matters related to Fixed Income affairs which require Legal team`s assessment or intervention.
